document_type: Emergency Department Discharge Papers - Ankle Sprain

medications.name: Ibuprofen
medications.instructions: Take 1 tablet by mouth every 6 hours as needed with food
medications.notes: Avoid if stomach ulcer, kidney disease, or blood thinner use.

medications.name: Acetaminophen
medications.instructions: Take 1 tablet by mouth every 6 hours as needed
medications.notes: Do not exceed 3,000 mg per day.

follow_up: Follow up with student health or sports medicine in 5 to 7 days if still unable to walk normally.
Begin gentle range-of-motion exercises after pain improves.
return_precautions: Return for numbness, toes turning blue or cold, rapidly worsening swelling, uncontrolled pain, new calf swelling, or inability to bear weight after several days.
risk_notes: Low to moderate risk: mobility limitation and follow-up if symptoms persist.
